statement.en: Inferring High-Level Events from Timestamped Data: Complexity and Medical Applications
statement.summary: In this paper, we develop a novel logic-based approach to detecting high-level temporally extended events from timestamped data and background knowledge. Our framework employs logical rules to capture existence and termination conditions for simple temporal events and to combine these into meta-events. In the medical domain, for example, disease episodes and therapies are inferred from timestamped clinical observations, such as diagnoses and drug administrations stored in patient records, and can be further combined into higher-level disease events. As some incorrect events might be inferred, we use constraints to identify incompatible combinations of events and propose a repair mechanism to select preferred consistent sets of events. While reasoning in the full framework is intractable, w

proposer: Yvon K. Awuklu, Meghyn Bienvenu, Katsumi Inoue et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint (dated 2026-04-24) describing a novel logic-based framework for temporal event inference. The core claims (soundness of the repair mechanism, completeness of event detection, practical medical applicability) remain unvalidated in peer review. The abstract truncates mid-sentence, suggesting incompleteness. Classification as FLOWING reflects provisional status pending publication and empirical validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間の粒から、矛盾なき事象の層が浮かび上がる。
